prevalence.source: WHO herpes simplex virus seroprevalence context; regional STI surveillance summaries
prevalence.trend: stable; HSV-2 higher in sexually active and high-risk groups

incidence.source: STI cohort syntheses and clinic surveillance

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; recurrent morbidity and psychosocial impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical lesions with confirmation by PCR/NAAT or culture when available; type-specific serology for unclear cases; assess risk in pregnancy

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; diagnosis is symptom-driven
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions (HSV-1 and HSV-2 combined)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to antivirals and counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; PCR confirmation available in reference labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acyclovir/valacyclovir episodic or suppressive therapy; counseling on transmission prevention; pregnancy management to reduce neonatal risk
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, underdiagnosis of asymptomatic infections, uneven access to PCR testing
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Antivirals available in public system and widely in private pharmacies
